commented_code:
# It is designed to run in ~4 hours on 8XH100 node at $3/GPU/hour.
 
 # 1) Example launch (simplest):
-# bash speedrun.sh
+# bash runs/speedrun.sh
 # 2) Example launch in a screen session (because the run takes ~4 hours):
-# screen -L -Logfile speedrun.log -S speedrun bash speedrun.sh
+# screen -L -Logfile speedrun.log -S speedrun bash runs/speedrun.sh
 # 3) Example launch with wandb logging, but see below for setting up wandb first:
-# WANDB_RUN=speedrun screen -L -Logfile speedrun.log -S speedrun bash speedrun.sh
+# WANDB_RUN=speedrun screen -L -Logfile speedrun.log -S speedrun bash runs/speedrun.sh
 
 # Default intermediate artifacts directory is in ~/.cache/nanochat
 export OMP_NUM_THREADS=1
 export NANOCHAT_BASE_DIR="$HOME/.cache/nanochat"
 mkdir -p $NANOCHAT_BASE_DIR
 
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# System dependencies (Python dev headers needed for Triton/torch compilation)
+
+if ! dpkg -s python3-dev &> /dev/null; then
+    echo "Installing python3-dev (required for Python.h)..."
+    sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y python3-dev
+fi
+

discussion_comments.comment_body: I'm a little confused by this addition and the PATH one below, as IIRC this script was just running out of the box on an 8xH100 for me (though that was a few days ago and I haven't tested on the very latest `master`)

discussion_comments.comment_body:
Sorry, I'm just seeing your comments. Yeah, I don't think I've had this issue on all instances, so it must have something to do with whether it's a container / virtual machine / bare metal instance?
I provision instances through primeintellect.ai, so I end up on different providers based on availability. I believe I was running on "Verda" for this one, which uses Virtual Machines. 
Without the above, the torch compiler would crash (with an error about python.h missing). 
I'm pretty bad at Linux and environment setup--I rely on AI to fumble my way through it. The changes you're pointing to are what Claude added to get me up and running. So if there's a cleaner way to handle them, great!

commented_code:
# Default intermediate artifacts directory is in ~/.cache/nanochat
 export OMP_NUM_THREADS=1
+# For newer AMD GPUs that are not yet officially supported by PyTorch ROCm builds,
+# we can override the detected GPU architecture to a compatible one.
+# For example, for a gfx1151 GPU, we can use gfx1100 (11.0.0).
+export HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION=11.0.0

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -12,6 +12,10 @@
 
 # Default intermediate artifacts directory is in ~/.cache/nanochat
 export OMP_NUM_THREADS=1
+# For newer AMD GPUs that are not yet officially supported by PyTorch ROCm builds,
+# we can override the detected GPU architecture to a compatible one.
+# For example, for a gfx1151 GPU, we can use gfx1100 (11.0.0).
+export HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION=11.0.0
discussion_comments.comment_body:
PyTorch should be supporting gfx1151 already via. TheRock. This shouldn't be enabled by default IMO.
This could also cause issues when running on gfx9 which @jon-hotaisle was running on.

+echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc
